summary: To determine interstitial fluid volume effectively, use a radioisotope-bound protein like labeled albumin because it distributes well within both blood vessels and tissues; this method ensures comprehensive measurement through principles similar to those used in the indicator-dilution technique. \boxed{\text{Albumin-based approach}} Summary: For assessing interstitial fluid volume, utilizing radiolabeled albumin is optimal since it evenly covers both vasculature and tissue areas, providing precise results akin to the indicator-dilation technique. This approach leverages albumin's consistent movement between these spaces. \boxed{\text{Albumin-based approach}}
